name: Zou

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Zou (zouu1235).

notes: Skeleton. Zou (Zomi) is a Kuki-Chin (Sino-Tibetan) language of Manipur and Chin State, written in a Latin script developed by J.H. Cope. Base Latin consonants and vowels are mapped; per the orthography ch is the palatal stop [c], and ng/kh/ph/th are the expected digraphs and aspirates. Lexical tone is not recovered by the romanization and is left unencoded, so an elevated PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Zou (zouu1235)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
